changeReason: Improved translation from Japanese. Old: "After the Great Disaster"

TextENGNew: ``...Um, excuse me. It's about time...``
The nurse watching the exchange near the entrance timidly called out to Ooishi and Akasaka. Akasaka looked over his shoulder and nodded, then put a hand on Ooishi's shoulder and spoke.
``Ooishi-san... let's leave it at that for today.``
``......I'm sorry. Please, just a little longer.``
Then, Ooishi pulled a plastic evidence bag out of his handbag. Inside of that... was a lightly stained beret.
``Mion Sonozaki-san... do you recognize this hat?``

Ooishi sat it down by Mion's knees in a rough gesture. That instant, the playing cards fell from Mion's hands. But Ooishi didn't focus on that, instead he moved in closer to gaze into Mion's eyes and inquire.
``You've seen it, haven't you? ...No, I'm certain you should remember it.@k@vS20/11/440700322. This is the hat your friend Rena Ryuugu-san always wore, isn't it?``

``This is it. It was found downstream in the river flowing through Hinamizawa. A fisherman found it inside a drifting wooden box.@k@vS20/11/440700324. It was found inside, along with the remains of a hamster.``
Whether Mion was listening to Ooishi's words or not, she was staring intently at the hat with a vacant look in her eyes.
Her expression was devoid of animation, as if her emotions had broken down. It was hard to tell if she would respond or not... but Ooishi steeled himself and called out to her with all his might.
``Do you understand? The fact that these were found together leads me to believe Rena Ryuugu-san was the one who cast this box into the river.``
``However, I don't understand why. But you should be able to understand the message behind this!@k@vS20/11/440700327. You were her friend. You were always playing together!``
``...Please, Mion Sonozaki-san...! Rena Ryuugu-san was trying to tell us something about what happened in Hinamizawa.``
``If we can unravel this, it might bring us one step closer to unraveling the secret behind the disaster!! So please, wake up!!``
``D-detective?!``
Ooishi put his hands on Mion's shoulders as his voice roared. The moment the nurse saw that, she pulled his oversized body away from Mion.
``Please, cease this violent behavior! Detective!!``
``Ooishi-san, please calm down...!``
Ooishi instinctively tried to shake the nurse off, but he came back to his senses after Akasaka stepped in... and then his shoulders flopped.
``Are you all right, Sonozaki-san? ...That's enough. Please, no more!@k@vS20/00/nurseA44005. Isn't that enough for today?!``
``...I'm sorry, I went too far. I lost control...``
``If you're going to behave this violently, I won't allow you to visit in the future! You're in a hospital. Don't you realize she's a patient?!@k@vS20/00/nurseA44007. Honestly...!``
``Nurse... this man has lost an incredible number of people in Hinamizawa. Please, try to understand how he feels.@k@vS20/13/441300064. ...I beg of you.``
The nurse was agitated, but Akasaka's intervention somehow managed to smooth things over. And then, when Akasaka turned to him and flashed a signal, Ooishi gently nodded.
``...Mion Sonozaki-san. I'll leave that hat here.@k@vS20/11/440700333. I'll be back tomorrow, so please tell me if you remember anything.@k@vS20/11/440700334. ...Alright, that's all.``
Then, Ooishi stepped out of the hospital room with Akasaka.
``Haah, unbelievable. Are you all right, Sonozaki-san...?``
``...Sonozaki-san?``
Mion was sitting silently, staring blankly at the hat resting by her knees, then suddenly raised her head and spoke up. The light in her eyes finally returned.
``Nurse, please! Call those detectives back here!@k@vS20/03/440300199. They couldn't have gotten far!!``
``W-what's going on, Sonozaki-san...? D-did you... get your memories back?!``
``I understand... I understand! What Rena was trying to tell us!@k@vS20/03/440300201. There was no disaster in Hinamizawa!@k@vS20/03/440300202. So please, bring them back right away!!``
``U-understood... P-please, wait a moment, ok...?``
``Rena... you gave your life to send this message, didn't you...? I received it... Yes, I absolutely did receive it...!!``
Mion held the hat in the plastic bag to her chest and tightly embraced it. And then, knowing the filthy stain on the hat was a bloodstain, she began spilling tears long thought lost to her...
Then the next morning, at 5:17 AM,
Mion Sonozaki was pronounced dead as a result of acute heart failure.
